target.name: Beyond Aero
target.founder: Eloa Guillotin, Hugo Tarlé, Valentin Chomel

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $44M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Beyond Aero, a Series A transportation-sector company founded by Eloa Guillotin, Hugo Tarlé, and Valentin Chomel, has raised $44M to date. The relevant comp universe comprises 8 private firms in Transportation, including Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Pipedream, and FleetZero; no public comparables exist in a narrow peer band.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable triangulation method here. Using total capital raised ($44M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $88M. Peer-median analysis cannot be deployed reliably: although 3 same-sector peers exist, none satisfy simultaneous filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or comparable valuation tier, leaving an insufficient dataset (minimum 3 required) for meaningful median derivation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $75M (low) to $101M (high), with a median point of $88M. This range reflects a single methodological input, creating material uncertainty. Key unknowns include whether Beyond Aero's capital efficiency and burn profile align with sector benchmarks, the specific transportation subsegment focus, and commercial traction to date. A more robust triangulation would require either expanded peer data within Transportation at comparable stages or introduction of alternative methods such as DCF or revenue multiple analysis, neither of which are currently available.
